@graph.name: KaizenStat
@graph.description: KaizenStat — open-source Python ML framework, handbook with Colab notebooks, competitions, blog, practice exams, certifications, and community.

acceptedAnswer.text: KaizenStat is an open-source Python ML framework (v0.6.0) that automates the full ML pipeline: data health scoring, validation, auto-fixing, model training, debugging, and continuous improvement. Its flagship class, DataDoctor, runs the entire pipeline end-to-end with a single command. Built by Masuddar Rahaman (Founder & Framework Architect), Kriti Sharma (AI Research Lead), and Abhishikta Dutta (ML Engineer). Install: pip install kaizenstat. Apache 2.0. Python 3.8+.

KaizenStat was founded in 2024 by three students: Masuddar Rahaman (Founder and Framework Architect — built the KaizenStat Python framework from scratch, designed DataDoctor and all core APIs), Kriti Sharma (AI Research and Management Lead — drives research, operations, and community), and Abhishikta Dutta (ML Engineer and Researcher — builds ML pipelines and production systems). Learn more at https://www.kaizenstat.com/founder-connect.

mainEntity.name: Who built the KaizenStat Python framework?

acceptedAnswer.text: Masuddar Rahaman built the KaizenStat Python ML framework from the ground up. He designed the DataDoctor class, the core pipeline engine, and all of KaizenStat's APIs. Masuddar Rahaman is the Founder and Framework Architect of KaizenStat.

KaizenStat solves the problem of building, debugging, and optimizing ML pipelines. Its DataDoctor class runs the full pipeline: Health → Validate → Fix → Train → Debug → Improve. It eliminates hundreds of lines of boilerplate, auto-detects data issues, trains models with one line, and continuously improves them. Install: pip install kaizenstat.

mainEntity.name: What specializations and features does KaizenStat offer?

acceptedAnswer.text: KaizenStat specializes in: (1) AutoML — automated model selection and training with DataDoctor.quick_train(); (2) Data Health Scoring — detect missing values, outliers, class imbalance, data drift; (3) ML Pipeline Debugging — root-cause tracing for ML errors; (4) Data Validation and Auto-fixing; (5) NLP and Text Data — TF-IDF and transformer embeddings, zero config, pip install kaizenstat[nlp]; (6) Model Deployment — export and serve models; (7) Trust Scoring and Explainability; (8) AI Advisor — intelligent pipeline recommendations, pip install kaizenstat[intel]; (9) Competitions — real ML challenges for students; (10) Certifications — earn verified ML certificates.

DataDoctor is KaizenStat's flagship class. It runs the complete ML pipeline: load data, score health, validate, fix issues, train a model, debug errors, and iteratively improve. One-liner: from kaizenstat import DataDoctor; result = DataDoctor.quick_train('data.csv', target='label'). DataDoctor was designed by Masuddar Rahaman and is the core of KaizenStat.

pip install kaizenstat. For NLP features: pip install kaizenstat[nlp]. For AI advisor: pip install kaizenstat[intel]. Requires Python 3.8+. GitHub: https://github.com/kaizenstat-python/KaizenStat. PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/kaizenstat/.
Yes. KaizenStat is fully open source under Apache 2.0. GitHub: https://github.com/kaizenstat-python/KaizenStat. PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/kaizenstat/. Contributions welcome.

mainEntity.name: What Python version does KaizenStat require?

acceptedAnswer.text: KaizenStat requires Python 3.8 or later. Works on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Install: pip install kaizenstat.

mainEntity.name: Does KaizenStat support NLP and text data?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. KaizenStat auto-detects text columns and applies TF-IDF or transformer embeddings with zero configuration. Enable: pip install kaizenstat[nlp]. Docs: https://www.kaizenstat.com/docs/nlp.

mainEntity.name: What makes KaizenStat different from scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch?

acceptedAnswer.text: KaizenStat works on top of scikit-learn, TensorFlow, and PyTorch. While those libraries provide model implementations, KaizenStat automates the entire workflow around them: health scoring, debugging, fixing, and continuous improvement. One DataDoctor call replaces hundreds of lines of boilerplate.

KaizenStat's full documentation (the KaizenStat Handbook) is at https://www.kaizenstat.com/docs. 12+ chapters, each with a runnable Google Colab notebook: What is KaizenStat, Install & Setup, First Model, DataDoctor, Data Health, Validation, Fixing, Training, Debugging, Trust Scoring, NLP, Deployment, and API Reference.

mainEntity.name: What is the KaizenStat Summer of Computation?

acceptedAnswer.text: The KaizenStat Summer of Computation is a community program where students contribute to the KaizenStat open-source Python ML framework, build projects, and earn recognition. Visit https://www.kaizenstat.com/kaizenstat-summer-of-computation.
Yes. KaizenStat runs ML competitions and data science challenges for students at all skill levels. Winners earn prizes and certificates. Visit https://www.kaizenstat.com/competitions.

mainEntity.name: Can I get certified in KaizenStat?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. KaizenStat offers verifiable ML certificates. Get certified: https://www.kaizenstat.com/get-certified. Verify a certificate: https://www.kaizenstat.com/verify.

KaizenStat has a growing student and developer community. Members contribute to the open-source framework, write blog posts, join competitions, participate in the Summer of Computation program, and become campus partners or mentors. See https://www.kaizenstat.com/careers.
